description: The shipped spawn-guard's rostered-spawn exemption requires both name and team_name, but the Agent/Task API deprecated team_name (a single implicit team now), so the conjunction is unsatisfiable and every rostered persistent-Teammate spawn falls through to deny. Rekey the exemption on a non-empty name (isNamedSpawn), drop team_name from the guard logic and the rules vocabulary, re-ground the deny premise on addressability rather than definition-less copy, and minimize the deny reason to point at .excn/TEAM_DIRECTIVE.md.
title: Realign spawn-guard to name-based addressability after team_name deprecation
acceptance_criteria: isNamedSpawn exempts a rostered spawn when tool_input.name is a non-empty string; team_name is removed from both the guard logic and the rules-module vocabulary
A rostered type spawned with a name passes (noop, logged); the same type spawned unnamed denies with the reason; both outcomes log one invocation record
DENY_REASON_TEMPLATE is the minimal form pointing to .excn/TEAM_DIRECTIVE.md, with no stale team_name instruction
The guard header premise is re-grounded on addressability/continuability, not definition-less copy
A node --test case covers named-pass and unnamed-deny for a rostered type
Src-First: src/template and root .claude/hooks spawn-guard.cjs and spawn-guard-rules.cjs are byte-identical
